{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox Film festival | name = 63rd Cannes Film Festival | image = 2010 Cannes Film Festival poster.jpg | caption = The festival's official poster featuring French actress [[Juliette Binoche]]{{cite web|url=http://photogallery.indiatimes.com/movies/film-festivals/cannes-film-festival-10-preparations/articleshow/5916579.cms |title=Cannes Film Festival '10: Preparations |publisher=Bennett, Coleman & Co. Ltd |work=The India Times |access-date=26 May 2017}} | opening = ''[[Robin Hood (2010 film)|Robin Hood]]'' | closing = ''[[The Tree (2010 film)|The Tree]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], [[France]] | founded = 1946 | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives]]'' | host = [[Kristin Scott Thomas]] | number = 19 (Main Competition) | date = 12–23 May 2010 | website = www.festival-cannes.com | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2009 Cannes Film Festival|2009]] | next = [[2011 Cannes Film Festival|2011]] }} The '''63rd [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' took place from 12 to 23 May 2010.{{cite web |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en.html |title=Festival de Cannes 2010 |access-date=26 January 2010 |publisher=Cannes Film Festival| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20160305014757/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/archives/2010/allSelections.html| archive-date= 5 March 2016 | url-status= live}}{{cite news |url=http://topics.nytimes.com/top/reference/timestopics/subjects/c/cannes_international_film_festival/index.html |title=Cannes International Film Festival |work=[[The New York Times]] |access-date=11 May 2010 | first=Manohla | last=Dargis| archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100520035530/http://topics.nytimes.com/top/reference/timestopics/subjects/c/cannes_international_film_festival/index.html| archive-date= 20 May 2010 | url-status= live}} American filmmaker [[Tim Burton]] served as jury president for the main competition.{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/8480146.stm |title=BBC News: Tim Burton to head Cannes film jury |work=BBC News | date=26 January 2010 |access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite news |url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2010/jan/26/tim-burton-heads-cannes-jury |title=Guardian: Tim Burton to head Cannes film festival jury |work=Guardian.co.uk |location=London |first=Peter |last=Bradshaw |access-date=26 May 2017}} Thai filmmaker [[Apichatpong Weerasethakul]] won the [[Palme d'Or]], the festival's top prize, for the drama film ''[[Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives]]''. English actress [[Kristin Scott Thomas]] was the mistress of ceremonies.{{cite web|url=https://deadline.com/2010/04/kristin-scott-thomas-is-cannes-mistress-of-ceremony-and-woody-will-be-there-too-31212/ |title=Kristin Scott Thomas Is Cannes Mistress Of Ceremony, And Woody Will Be There Too |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |access-date=26 May 2017}} [[Agence France-Presse]], [[Reuters]], [[Associated Press]] and Getty TV boycotted the press conference that announced the line-up for the festival, due to a dispute over access to the red carpet.{{cite news |url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/8619843.stm |title=Cannes row leads to press boycott |work=BBC News |publisher=BBC Online |date=14 April 2010 |access-date=26 May 2017}} In a press release, the agencies said that they "may be forced to suspend their presence at the festival altogether" if an agreement was not reached. Days before the festival was to begin, concerns were expressed that attendees might be delayed, or would not attend, due to plane flights to surrounding areas in France being delayed or canceled due to [[Air travel disruption after the 2010 Eyjafjallajökull eruption|volcanic ash in the sky]].{{cite news |url=http://latimesblogs.latimes.com/movies/2010/05/will-rusell-crowe-and-sean-penn-need-to-board-a-steamshipwith-the-cannes-film-festival-just-three-days-away-the-volcanic-as.html |title=An ash-colored pall could settle over the Cannes Film Festival |work=[[Los Angeles Times]] |publisher=[[Tribune Company]] |first=Steven |last=Zeitchik |date=9 May 2010 |access-date=26 May 2017}} The festival opened with ''[[Robin Hood (2010 film)|Robin Hood]]'' by [[Ridley Scott]],{{cite news |date=26 March 2010 |title=BBC News: Robin Hood launches Cannes Film Festival |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/10109692 |access-date=26 May 2017 |work=BBC News |publisher=BBC Online}} and closed with ''[[The Tree (2010 film)|The Tree]]'' by [[Julie Bertuccelli]].{{cite web |title=63rd Festival de Cannes: Press Conference |url=http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/57145.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121014232133/http://www.festival-cannes.com/en/article/57145.html |archive-date=14 October 2012 |access-date=11 May 2010 |publisher=Cannes Film Festival}} [[File:Apichatpong.jpg|thumb|245x245px|[[Apichatpong Weerasethakul]], winner of the 2010 Palme d'Or]] [[File:Xavier Beauvois 2011.jpg|thumb|241x241px|[[Xavier Beauvois]], winner of the 2010 Gran Prix]] [[File:MahametSalehHarounCannesMay10.jpg|thumb|200x200px|[[Mahamat Saleh Haroun|Mahamat-Saleh Haroun]] at the festival for his film ''[[A Screaming Man]]'']]The [[Palme d'Or]] was won by the [[Thailand|Thai]] film ''[[Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives]]'' directed by [[Apichatpong Weerasethakul]].{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/2/hi/entertainment/8699394.stm |title=Thai film pulls off Cannes shock |date=23 May 2010 |work=BBC News |access-date=26 May 2017}} It was the first time that an Asian movie won the award since 1997.{{cite news |url=http://goldderby.latimes.com/awards_goldderby/2010/05/cannes-film-festival-uncle-boonmee-thailand-javier-bardem-winners-film-news.html |title=Quelle surprise! 'Uncle Boonmee' nabs Palme d'Or at Cannes |last=O'Neil |first=Tom |date=23 May 2010 |work=Los Angeles Times |access-date=26 May 2017 |archive-date=25 May 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100525070632/http://goldderby.latimes.com/awards_goldderby/2010/05/cannes-film-festival-uncle-boonmee-thailand-javier-bardem-winners-film-news.html |url-status=dead }} [[Tim Burton]], chairman of the jury that determined the award, stated about its decision: "You always want to be surprised by films and this film did that for most of us".{{cite news |url=http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/article7134539.ece |title=Thai film wins Palme d'Or as Ken Loach and Mike Leigh go home empty handed |last=Hoyle |first=Ben |date=23 May 2010 |work=The Times |access-date=23 May 2010 |location=London |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110615101556/http://entertainment.timesonline.co.uk/tol/arts_and_entertainment/film/article7134539.ece |archive-date=15 June 2011 }} French film ''[[Of Gods and Men (film)|Of Gods and Men]]'' was the runner up.{{cite news |url=http://news.blogs.cnn.com/2010/05/23/thai-film-grabs-top-prize-at-cannes/ |title=Thai film grabs top prize at Cannes |date=23 May 2010 |work=CNN News |access-date=26 May 2017 |archive-date=26 May 2010 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100526033726/http://news.blogs.cnn.com/2010/05/23/thai-film-grabs-top-prize-at-cannes/ |url-status=dead }} The [[Xavier Beauvois]]' film had been considered a favorite alongside [[Mike Leigh]]'s ''[[Another Year (film)|Another Year]]''.{{cite news|url=http://abcnews.go.com/Entertainment/wireStory?id=10723555|title=Thai Film Surprise Winner in Cannes|last=Collett-White|first=Mike|author2=Mackenzie, James|date=23 May 2010|work=ABC News|accessdate=23 May 2010| archiveurl= https://web.archive.org/web/20100526153034/http://abcnews.go.com/Entertainment/wireStory?id=10723555| archivedate= 26 May 2010 | url-status=live}} During the ceremony special attention was paid to Iranian filmmaker [[Jafar Panahi]] in hopes of increasing international pressure on the Iranian government to release Panahi from jail. The following films and people received the 2010 Official selection awards:{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2010/allAward.html |title=Awards 2010: All Awards |work=festival-cannes.fr |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120204152623/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2010/allAward.html |archive-date=4 February 2012 }}{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2010.htm |title=63ème Festival de Cannes |work=cinema-francais.fr |access-date=24 July 2017}} === In Competition === * [[Palme d'Or]]: ''[[Uncle Boonmee Who Can Recall His Past Lives]]'' by [[Apichatpong Weerasethakul]] * [[Grand Prix (Cannes Film Festival)|Grand Prix]]: '' [[Of Gods and Men (film)|Of Gods and Men]]'' by [[Xavier Beauvois]] * [[Best Director Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Director]]: [[Mathieu Amalric]] for ''[[On Tour (2010 film)|On Tour]]'' * [[Best Screenplay Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Screenplay]]: ''[[Poetry (film)|Poetry]]'' by [[Lee Chang-dong]] * [[Best Actress Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actress]]: [[Juliette Binoche]] for ''[[Certified Copy (film)|Certified Copy]]'' * [[Best Actor Award (Cannes Film Festival)|Best Actor]]: ** [[Javier Bardem]] for ''[[Biutiful]]'' ** [[Elio Germano]] for ''[[La nostra vita|Our Life]]'' * [[Jury Prize (Cannes Festival)|Prix du Jury]]: ''[[A Screaming Man]]'' by [[Mahamat Saleh Haroun|Mahamat-Saleh Haroun]] === Un Certain Regard === * [[Un Certain Regard|Prix Un Certain Regard]]: ''[[Hahaha]]'' by [[Hong Sang-soo]] * '''Un Certain Regard Jury Prize''': Fifth Column (film)|The Fifth Column]]'' by Vatche Boulghourjian ** ''I Already Am Everything I Want to Have'' by Dane Komljen === Golden Camera === * [[Caméra d'Or]]: ''[[Año Bisiesto]]'' by
